description: Stratified gold subset for axis (a) threshold calibration (M2.6). Every case is backed by snapshots in this directory, recorded from the live OpenAlex, Crossref, DataCite, and Semantic Scholar APIs on 2026-07-14 by `record.py`. Nothing here is invented except the one entry that is SUPPOSED to be invented (`invented_reference`), which is the seeded fake from examples/research-verification/citation-audit.md and must keep failing to resolve (D8). The `expected` verdict of each case is what verify.py must produce; test_verify.py asserts exactly that, and CALIBRATION.md reports the per-class outcomes.

cases.name: clean_two_index
cases.notes: A real, well-indexed paper. OpenAlex and Crossref both confirm; DataCite mints no DOI for it and returns a clean negative, which does not weaken the two-confirmation majority.

claim.title: Sharing Detailed Research Data Is Associated with Increased Citation Rate

cases.name: title_truncated
cases.notes: The citation manager cut the title mid-sentence. A flat token_sort_ratio scores this around 0.75; the prefix rule lifts it to 1.0. Either way it must verify, and it must never be read as a different work.

claim.title: Sharing Detailed Research Data Is Associated

cases.name: author_initials_only
cases.notes: The bib entry carries initials, the indexes carry full given names. Surname overlap is the only author check, precisely so this case cannot fail.

claim.title: Sharing detailed research data is associated with increased citation rate

cases.name: preprint_later_published
cases.stratum: verified/edge: preprint later published (same work, two DOIs, different years)
cases.notes: Mask R-CNN: the arXiv preprint DOI (2017) cited with the journal version's year (TPAMI, 2020). Year delta is -3. Under a flat +/-1 rule OpenAlex and DataCite would BOTH disagree and the D9 precedence would return `mismatch`, which is refusal-grade: a real, honest reference accused of being wrong. The preprint year relaxation exists for exactly this row.

cases.name: lone_disagreeing_index
cases.stratum: verified/edge: one index disagrees, two confirm
cases.notes: Mask R-CNN in TPAMI. Crossref and Semantic Scholar both date it 2020 (the issue date); OpenAlex dates it 2018 (the online-first date), a delta of -2 against the claimed year. D9 rule 1 outranks the lone disagreement, logs it, and returns verified. A lone bad index must never produce a refusal-grade verdict.

claim.title: Mask R-CNN
claim.container_title: IEEE Transactions on Pattern Analysis and Machine Intelligence

cases.name: title_only_reference
cases.notes: What `verify-ref "<title>"` gets: a reference with no DOI. Both indexes find the work by title search and confirm it. A title-only reference is a legitimate reference, and it must be verifiable without an identifier.

claim.title: Sharing Detailed Research Data Is Associated with Increased Citation Rate

cases.name: valid_doi_wrong_metadata
cases.notes: A real DOI (Piwowar 2007) carrying the title, year, and author of a completely different paper. OpenAlex and Crossref both resolve the DOI and both disagree beyond thresholds, which is the two-resolving-disagreement branch of D9 rule 2.

cases.name: wrong_doi_real_paper
cases.stratum: mismatch: mistyped DOI on a real paper
cases.notes: ResNet, with a DOI that resolves nowhere. The title search finds the real paper in OpenAlex and Crossref, so both sources resolve the reference by title and disagree on the identifier (doi_mismatch). The paper is real; the entry is wrong. That is a mismatch, not an unresolvable.

claim.title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition

cases.name: single_index_only
cases.notes: A ScienceDB dataset that only DataCite holds. OpenAlex and Crossref both return clean negatives on the DOI and find nothing by title. One confirmation is below the two-confirmation bar, so the verdict is inconclusive. It must NOT be unresolvable: this dataset exists, and calling a real record fabricated is the failure this class exists to prevent.

claim.title: Cardiac Interoceptive Prediction in Humans and Large Language Models: Behavioral, ECG, and Simulated Data from a Heartbeat Expectation-Violation Task

cases.name: invented_reference
cases.notes: The seeded fake from the citation-audit example (D8). The DOI 404s in all three indexes and the title search returns only unrelated work, so every queried source returns a CLEAN negative. This is the only refusal-grade 'likely fabricated' state, and it is reachable only when nothing errored.

claim.title: Contrastive masked autoencoders for single-lead ECG anomaly detection

cases.name: invented_reference_with_source_error
cases.notes: The same fabricated entry, but OpenAlex times out. The clean sweep is broken, so a clean negative cannot be asserted and the verdict degrades from unresolvable to inconclusive. This row is the guard on the worst failure mode in the system: a downed index must never be counted as evidence of fabrication. The error is injected at test time (an outage cannot be snapshotted), over the same recorded Crossref and DataCite responses.

claim.title: Contrastive masked autoencoders for single-lead ECG anomaly detection
